_comment: CALMaR webapp asset registry. Asset IDs are globally unique across modelAssets/atlasAssets/connectomeAssets/annotationAssets so stages and result interpreters can reference them unambiguously. checksum/sizeBytes are placeholders until the assets are uploaded to the Hugging Face dataset repo.

pipelines.displayName: Auto Yeo overlap (T1 -> SynthStrip -> seg -> MNI -> Yeo)

modelAssets._comment: Phase 2a.1 brain-extraction model. SynthStrip ONNX export converted from FreeSurfer's main synthstrip.1.pt checkpoint, not the no-CSF or pediatric variants; conversion script was ported from neurodesk/vesselboost-webapp@web/models/synthstrip.onnx (FreeSurfer Apache-2.0). Wrapper code uses LIA reorientation + 1mm resample + 192-320 conform + 99th percentile normalization + SDT<1 threshold + largest-CC + interior fill (matches FreeSurfer defaults). WASM execution provider only — WebGPU lacks 3D MaxPool.

Phase 2a.2 lesion-segmentation model. Exported from liamchalcroft/synthstroke-baseline (MELBA 2025; MIT) — the baseline 3D MONAI UNet for T1w input with classes background/stroke. Upstream model card uses 192^3 patches, 1mm spacing, optional TTA; CALMaR exports the same safetensors weights to a browser-bounded static-128^3 ONNX and runs sliding-window overlap 0.25 WITH 8-axis test-time augmentation. TTA was added to suppress reflection-unstable false positives (notably in-brain cerebellum/posterior-fossa clusters); on the prealigned MNI160 input it removes the cerebellar FP while preserving ~95% of the true lesion. Raising overlap (0.5) only costs recall; the upstream 192^3 graph is both worse here and not browser-shippable from the static-128 export. Forward output is 2-channel logits (background, stroke); worker collapses stroke-background logit before sigmoid/thresholding.

DeepISLES browser-seed candidate only. This NVAUTO single-fold ONNX is too large for the current cold-load budget and remains supportStatus=benchmark-only until the Dice 0.5 gap analysis identifies a browser-feasible candidate. The app exposes an opt-in DWI/ADC seed hook but refuses to run this asset while it is not marked supported.

Phase 3 MNI registration. Exported from voxelmorph/synthmorph 'brains-dice-vel-0.5-res-16-256f.h5' (Hoffmann et al. 2022, Apache-2.0). Only the UNet backbone (layers 0-33 of the full network) is in the ONNX — output is the raw stationary velocity field at half-resolution. The browser model reuses the original SynthMorph weights on a 48x64x80 static graph so the largest first-conv activation is 240 MiB instead of ~4.7 GiB. The graph still contains 3D MaxPool nodes, so the worker routes it through WASM because ORT WebGPU does not support NHWC 3D pooling. The worker downsamples the MNI160 source/reference pair to 48x64x80, runs ONNX, then performs scaling-and-squaring SVF integration, 24x32x40 -> 160x160x192 displacement upsample, and spatial warp in pure JS (web/js/modules/registration.js); see scripts/convert_registration_model.py.

atlasAssets._comment: Phase 3 SynthMorph registration target. The 'brains' SynthMorph model was trained at 160x160x192 1mm; the orchestrator hands this reference + the source T1 to the model and gets back a stationary velocity field. Built by resampling nilearn's MNI152NLin2009cAsym 1mm template onto a 160x160x192 grid centred on the brain, then min-max normalised to [0,1]. Shares the lnm-models cache name space; lives under atlasAssets so it can use the existing atlas-loader path.

Phase 1 atlas. Yeo 2011 7-network liberal-mask parcellation, resampled (nearest-neighbour) from FreeSurfer-conformed 1mm to MNI152NLin2009cAsym 2mm via nilearn. Labels 1..7 map to canonical Yeo network names; 0 is background. Hosted under sbollmann/lnm-webapp-models pending neurodesk org access; transfer planned.

Schaefer 2018 400-parcel 7-network cortical parcellation at FSL MNI152 2mm. Labels 1..400 map to Schaefer parcel names with the leading 7Networks_ display prefix removed; networkLabels provide the corresponding Yeo 7-network membership for grouping.
Derived nearest-neighbor 4mm Schaefer atlas for labeling Schaefer FC maps. Generated by scripts/build_schaefer400_connectome.py when the 4mm FC pack is built.

connectomeAssets._comment: Phase 38 group-FC pack. 7 brain-wide one-sample t-maps (one per Yeo network) stacked Float32 in NumPy row-major order; decodeFcPack transposes channels to NIfTI x-fast order before thresholding or display. Computed by scripts/build_yeo7_connectome.py from development_fmri 155 subjects via nilearn fetch_development_fmri (Richardson 2018, movie-watching task, CC0 — no DUT required). Per-subject Yeo7 ROI mean -> Pearson r-map vs every brain voxel -> Fisher-z -> group t-stat. Same 99x117x95 2mm grid as the yeo7-2mm atlas. Group t-stat range observed [-13.47, 30.72] (vs [-13.85, 19.74] for the prior ADHD-200 N=40 pack — peak +stat ~55% higher with the larger sample).

Schaefer400 public-development-fMRI FC pack. 400 parcel-seed group t-maps stored as lazy float16 row-major shards so the browser fetches only parcels hit by the lesion. Computed by scripts/build_schaefer400_connectome.py from development_fmri 155 subjects via nilearn fetch_development_fmri. Group t-stat range observed [-15.07, 55.12].

annotationAssets._comment: Phase 40 exploratory functional association profiles. Compact browser JSON keyed by Yeo7 network name; generated/rebuilt offline with scripts/build_yeo7_function_profiles.py from Neurosynth v7 term annotations through NiMARE, then combined at runtime with direct lesion-overlap and thresholded connectivity-map network weights. Intended for exploratory interpretation only, not clinical prediction.

Exploratory Schaefer400 functional association profiles. Compact browser JSON keyed by Schaefer parcel label with the leading 7Networks_ display prefix removed; generated with scripts/build_schaefer400_function_profiles.py from Neurosynth v7 abstract term TF-IDF annotations through NiMARE ROIAssociationDecoder, with each Schaefer parcel decoded as its own ROI. Intended for exploratory interpretation only, not clinical prediction.
